title: BAU 3b matrix step 1 tightening — replace severity-as-escalation-trigger with operator-only-judgment criteria; preserve M1/M3 conservative posture; routing matrix gains a context-aware step 1.
amends: monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md — step 1 of the 5-action decision tree gains three invocation-context branches (M1 stale-sweep, M3 carry-over, BAU 3b). M1 and M3 branches preserve the existing conservative invariant. BAU 3b branch routes by the operator-only-judgment-criteria check, not by severity tier. New 'Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria' subsection enumerates the five categories LLM checks against in BAU context.

monitor/prompts/decider.md — Priority 3b BAU triage section gains a sentence pointing decider to the BAU branch of step 1 (one-line addition: 'When invoking the matrix from this step, use step 1's BAU branch — see routing-matrix.md "Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria".'). No structural change.
target_files: monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md (step 1 restructure + new 'Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria' subsection; agent-applied prompt edit)
monitor/prompts/decider.md (one-line pointer in Priority 3b; agent-applied prompt edit)

executive_summary.problem_one_line: PROP-031 BAU 3b's first run (2026-05-12T05:12Z) over-escalated 7 of 7 major/critical items because matrix step 1 'severity ∈ {major, critical} → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN' fires uniformly across invocation contexts. Operator manually rerouted all 7 to assigned-analyst with class_hints. Severity tier is not a reliable proxy for 'requires operator-only judgment' — citation precision and analyst defense work are analyst-class regardless of severity.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Restructure matrix step 1 into three invocation-context branches. M1 stale-sweep and M3 carry-over branches preserve the existing severity-driven invariant (correct for those contexts). BAU 3b branch replaces severity check with a five-category operator-only-judgment check; severity becomes class_hint input only. Land 'Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria' as enumerated, concrete checks (not vague judgment) so Sonnet decider applies them consistently.

tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q1_design_choice: Tighten ONLY BAU 3b's step 1; preserve M1 and M3 step 1 unchanged. Rationale: M1 and M3 operate on a different item population (items that survived earlier triage in M1's case; items curmudgeon already escalated as carry-over in M3's case), where the conservative-default-escalate posture is empirically defensible. BAU 3b operates on freshly-raised items where the description shape, not severity, indicates routing target. Three-context step 1 keeps the decision tree single-page-readable while honoring the operator-only-judgment principle the directive identifies.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q2_criteria_format: Enumerated five-category list, NOT 'use judgment' instruction. Sonnet (decider's current model) is more reliable with explicit checks than open-ended judgment. The five categories: (1) verdict commit on already-published WIN with operator authorship in verdict_history, (2) NEVER_PUSH file modification, (3) legal/strategic/external-communication content, (4) physical-world verification or operator-side knowledge, (5) pipeline-config change. Each criterion includes a one-line trigger phrase and a worked example. The PROP also enumerates four explicitly-NOT-operator-only categories (citation precision, strawman defense, narrow precision, verdict-change PROPOSAL drafting) so Sonnet has concrete examples on both sides.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q3_severity_demotion: Severity is not removed from the matrix — it remains useful as a class_hint default (critical/major usually → 'deep-attack'; minor/info usually → 'verification' for narrow-fix work). The change is: severity no longer determines the escalate-vs-route decision in BAU 3b. Severity informs HOW we route, not WHETHER we escalate. M1 and M3 still use severity as the escalate trigger because the item-population cuts differently (see Q1).
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q4_retroactive_simulation_outcome: All 7 items from the 2026-05-12T05:12Z over-escalation batch (ISS-1921, 1933, 1944, 1945, 1948, 1957, 1958) route correctly to analyst with appropriate class_hints under the proposed BAU step 1. Zero escalate-to-human. Routing matches the operator's manual reroute exactly. Detailed walk-through in retroactive_simulation section below.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q5_safety_net: If decider's LLM is uncertain whether an item matches an operator-only category, the PROP's tie-breaker is: ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN (false-positive escalation > false-negative escalation). The cost of escalating an analyst-class item is one operator-reroute (~30 seconds); the cost of routing an operator-only item to analyst is the analyst running on something that isn't its work, which may itself eventually re-escalate (~one analyst run wasted). False-positive is cheaper. The criterion examples make 'clear analyst-class' obvious; ambiguous items still escalate.

executive_summary.scope_clarification: This PROP authorizes design only. Both edits are agent-applied prompt edits (monitor/prompts/*.md is git-owned, agent-applied via decider self-apply or workspace-sync universal-push; see CLAUDE.md File Ownership). Operator reviews; on approval, decider self-applies the two edits in next run. PROP-027 (the routing matrix) and PROP-031 (BAU 3b) are NOT replaced — this PROP refines step 1 of PROP-027 when invoked from PROP-031's context.

context_verification.investigation_method: Read DIRECTIVE-20260512-001 in full; read monitor/prompts/reference/routing-matrix.md (current step 1 wording L33-34 — single-row 'severity ∈ {major, critical} → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Invariant. Never auto-close major/critical.'); read PROP-027 (matrix origin, L71-77 step 1 rationale); read PROP-031 (BAU 3b extension, L142-159 — confirmed 3b defers to routing-matrix.md without overriding step 1); examined the 7 over-escalated items in open-issues.json + closed-issues.json (ISS-1921/1933/1944/1945/1948/1957/1958 — pulled status, severity, source, routing_reason, class_hint, full description); ran retroactive simulation manually against proposed step 1 BAU branch.

first_BAU_3b_run_audit.operator_verdict: ZERO of 7 genuinely require operator-only judgment

major_citation_or_precision: ISS-1958 (WIN-014.c7 H2 Crossley URL year mismatch)
major_verdict_reclassification_proposal: ISS-1921 (WIN-034 SC reclassification — analyst drafts EXP, operator approves at integration)

directive_claim_validation.operator_claim_2026_05_12T05_30Z: of the 7 escalated, ZERO genuinely require operator-only judgment
directive_claim_validation.tinker_independent_verification: Confirmed by reading each ISS description and routing_reason field. Three are citation-fix work (verifiable by analyst fetching URL). Two are strawman-defense work (analyst writes EXP). One is narrow precision patch with named-station cite (analyst verification class). One is verdict-reclassification PROPOSAL (analyst drafts EXP; operator's role is at integration time, not at proposal-drafting time — separable steps). Operator's claim is empirically supported.

current_pipeline_state_at_tinker_run.interpretation: BAU 3b's mechanical work is succeeding (open status drained from 48 → 1; oldest open age 7.83d → 0.39d). The bug is now isolated to step 1's escalation discipline, not throughput. PROP-032 is the targeted fix.

step_1_invariant_origin_audit.PROP_027_introduction: Step 1 'severity ∈ {major, critical} → ESCALATE' was introduced 2026-05-10 in PROP-027 as a PROP-026-era safety net for the M1 stale-sweep. PROP-026 era had no analyst-routing path; the matrix had three actions (PATCH/WONTFIX/ESCALATE) and major/critical defaulting to ESCALATE was correct under that constraint.
step_1_invariant_origin_audit.PROP_027_extension: PROP-027 added ROUTE-TO-ANALYST and ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON, BUT preserved step 1 unchanged because PROP-027's deployment context was still M1 stale-sweep (no BAU 3b yet). The conservative posture was correct for the M1 launch — over-escalation on a freshly-deployed routing path was the safer failure mode.
step_1_invariant_origin_audit.PROP_031_extension: PROP-031 (2026-05-11) added BAU 3b as a third invocation context, deferring entirely to routing-matrix.md without overriding step 1. This was the implicit decision point — should step 1 differ across contexts, or be uniform? PROP-031 chose uniform. First-run evidence (2026-05-12) shows uniform was wrong. PROP-032 corrects.

operator_only_judgment_criteria.introduction: Five concrete categories that genuinely require operator-only knowledge or operator-only authority. If a BAU 3b candidate matches ANY of these, ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. If it matches NONE, fall through to step 2 of the matrix (route per remaining steps 2-6).

five_categories.name: Verdict commit on operator-authored published material
five_categories.trigger: Item proposes changing the verdict field on a WIN where verdict_history shows the most recent verdict change was authored by the operator (not by an EXP integration).
five_categories.rationale: PROP-028's verdict_history invariant preserves operator discretion on verdict commits. Analyst can DRAFT a verdict-change EXP, but the commit itself is operator territory. CRITICAL DISTINCTION: verdict-change PROPOSAL drafting (analyst writes the EXP) is NOT in this category — only the actual verdict_field mutation is.
five_categories.example_in_scope: Item: 'WIN-021 verdict should flip from Confirmed to Refuted by Data per analyst's EXP-NNN.' If WIN-021's last verdict_history entry is operator-authored → escalate. If last entry is EXP-integration → analyst can integrate next EXP without escalation.
five_categories.example_out_of_scope: Item: 'WIN-034 may warrant SC reclassification — analyst should draft EXP.' This is PROPOSAL drafting, NOT verdict commit. Route to analyst class='holistic'.

five_categories.name: NEVER_PUSH file modification required
five_categories.trigger: Item describes a fix that requires modifying any file in the workspace-sync NEVER_PUSH list (build.js, build-scripts/*, test.js, monitor/prompts/*.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/*.html when not derivable from sections.json).
five_categories.rationale: NEVER_PUSH files are operator-applied per CLAUDE.md File Ownership; agents cannot push edits to them via the normal commit path. Analyst routing would just bounce back.
five_categories.example_in_scope: Item: 'test.js needs new assertion for verdict_history invariant.' → escalate.
five_categories.example_out_of_scope: Item: 'docs/index.html prose section 6.14 is wrong.' If the prose lives in sections.json (which is git-owned, agent-pushable), route to analyst, not escalate. The NEVER_PUSH gate per routing-matrix.md narrowness gate's NOT_NEVER_PUSH sub-rule already covers this.

five_categories.name: Legal, strategic, or external-communication content
five_categories.trigger: Item touches the Timestamp Error tab framing (canary), public-facing scoreboard wording, FAQ tone, or any content that is operator's strategic decision (e.g., 'should we publish the dome-author DM contact as on-site evidence?').
five_categories.rationale: These touch project-strategy considerations the operator owns; analyst is not authorized to reframe project posture.
five_categories.example_in_scope: Item: 'Timestamp Error tab should be reframed as more confrontational.' → escalate.
five_categories.example_out_of_scope: Item: 'Section 6.14 prose has a citation error.' Citation correctness is analyst-class. Route to analyst.

five_categories.name: Physical-world verification or operator-side knowledge required
five_categories.trigger: Item requires consulting external instrument data the operator owns, dome-author DM context, or operator-side knowledge not in the workspace.
five_categories.rationale: Analyst cannot fetch operator's local instrument data or DM history. The directive's example (ISS-1924 'physical figure verification') is canonical.
five_categories.example_in_scope: Item: 'WIN-NN claim depends on operator-side measurement we haven't published.' → escalate.
five_categories.example_out_of_scope: Item: 'WIN-013 cite has wrong DOI.' Analyst fetches the correct DOI from CrossRef or DOI resolver — public-data verification, not operator-side. Route to analyst class='verification'.

five_categories.name: Pipeline-config change
five_categories.trigger: Item proposes changing decider-mode.json, scheduled-task cadence, agent model assignment, or any pipeline-control file.
five_categories.rationale: Pipeline-control files are operator-managed; agents propose via PROP/HNOTE, operator flips the actual config.
five_categories.example_in_scope: Item: 'Schedule cadence should flip from BAU 21d to burndown 7d per backlog growth.' → escalate.
five_categories.example_out_of_scope: Item: 'Tinker should add a new metric to queue-history.jsonl.' This is a PROP-authoring task; route to analyst (or this is already a tinker-authored PROP — out of scope for BAU 3b entirely).

explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.name: Citation precision (DOI/URL wrong, paper resolves elsewhere)
explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.rationale: Analyst fetches the correct citation from public databases (CrossRef, DOI resolver, journal archives) and patches wins.json or sections.json. Severity irrelevant — a critical citation failure is still verifiable mechanical work.

explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.name: Strawman / steelman defense (curmudgeon raised attribution risk; analyst writes counter-defense)
explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.rationale: Analyst Mode 2 (assigned-analyst defense) is the standard handler for curmudgeon-raised concerns. Severity irrelevant.

explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.name: Narrow-precision wording fix (single-line or single-paragraph clarification with named source)
explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.rationale: Analyst can apply this as a verification-class EXP with the named cite. The narrowness gate (matrix narrowness gate, three-rule conjunctive) may catch this as NARROW-PATCH, but if not, route to analyst is correct — not escalate.

explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.name: Verdict-change PROPOSAL drafting (analyst writes EXP; operator weighs in at integration)
explicitly_NOT_operator_only_categories.rationale: The PROPOSAL is analyst-class work. The COMMIT (when EXP lands) is operator-class per OOJ-1. These are temporally separated and the BAU 3b decision point is at proposal-drafting time, not commit time.

operator_only_judgment_criteria.tie_breaker_when_uncertain: If decider's LLM is genuinely uncertain whether an item matches one of the OOJ categories, default to ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Rationale: false-positive escalation costs ~30s of operator-reroute (well-known recovery path). False-negative escalation costs an analyst run on the wrong work + eventual re-escalation. False-positive is cheaper. The five OOJ examples and four NOO examples should make most cases unambiguous; uncertainty should be rare.

step_1_three_branch_redesign.current_routing_matrix_md_step_1_text: | 1 | severity ∈ {major, critical} | **ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN** | Invariant. Never auto-close major/critical. |
step_1_three_branch_redesign.proposed_routing_matrix_md_step_1_text:
Step 1 becomes context-aware. The single matrix table is preserved for steps 2-6 (unchanged). Step 1 is replaced by a three-branch subsection above the table:

**Step 1 — Escalate-to-Human (context-aware):**

| Invocation Context | Step 1 Rule | Action |
|---|---|---|
| **M1 stale-sweep** (Priority 5b, age ≥ N_DAYS) | severity ∈ {major, critical} | ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Invariant — never auto-close major/critical that survived all earlier triage; M1 by definition catches items that may be operator-territory. |
| **M3 carry-over** (decider-curmudgeon Step 8c, per-curmudgeon-review unresolved_prior_cycle) | severity ∈ {major, critical} AND no prior route action this cycle | ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Invariant — items curmudgeon already flagged twice without resolution may be operator-territory. |
| **BAU 3b** (Priority 3b, status='open' age ≥ 12h) | Description matches one of the five Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria (see subsection below) | ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Severity tier informs class_hint but NOT escalate-vs-route decision. If no OOJ category matches, fall through to step 2. |

For M1 and M3, severity is the escalation trigger (preserves PROP-027 invariant). For BAU 3b, the Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria are the trigger; severity informs class_hint downstream.
step_1_three_branch_redesign.preserved_invariants: Steps 2-6 of the matrix are unchanged. The narrowness gate is unchanged. The class-hint propagation chain is unchanged. The closure-ledger schema is unchanged (still uses action_taken='escalate' for both severity-based and OOJ-based escalations; closure_evidence.rationale captures which triggered).

decider_md_priority_3b_pointer_addition.insertion_point: monitor/prompts/decider.md Priority 3b section (added by PROP-031). Within the per_item_procedure subsection.
decider_md_priority_3b_pointer_addition.existing_text_excerpt: Apply the routing-matrix.md 5-action decision tree (same as M1 Priority 5b and M3 carry-over): PATCH | NARROW-PATCH | WONTFIX-WITH-RATIONALE | ROUTE-TO-ANALYST | ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON | ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN.
decider_md_priority_3b_pointer_addition.addition: Append: 'When invoking step 1, use the BAU branch — see routing-matrix.md "Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria" subsection. Severity tier is class_hint input only, NOT the escalation trigger in BAU context. M1 (Priority 5b) and M3 (Step 8c) continue to use the severity-based step 1.'
decider_md_priority_3b_pointer_addition.rationale: One-line pointer ensures Sonnet decider knows which branch of step 1 applies when running 3b. Without it, Sonnet might default to the M1-era reading (severity=trigger) since that's the historical posture the matrix has always carried.

design_specification.ledger_schema_no_change: closure-ledger.jsonl schema unchanged. Both severity-based escalations (M1/M3) and OOJ-based escalations (BAU 3b) use action_taken='escalate' with closure_evidence.rationale field carrying the specific trigger. Tinker's BAU-vs-M1-vs-M3 split-by-mechanism reporting (already in PROP-027/031) continues to work.

retroactive_simulation.introduction: Re-walk each of the 7 over-escalated items from 2026-05-12T05:12Z under the proposed BAU step 1. Per directive task_4: expected outcome is all 7 route to analyst with appropriate class_hints, zero escalate-to-human, matching operator's manual reroute exactly.

items.description_summary: WIN-034 cast-copper firmament SC reclassification proposal. Analyst drafts EXP; operator approves verdict commit when EXP lands.
items.OOJ_check: OOJ-1 (verdict commit)? NO — this is PROPOSAL drafting, not verdict commit. NOO-4 explicitly covers this. Other OOJ categories don't match (no NEVER_PUSH file, no legal/strategic content, no physical-world data, no pipeline-config).
items.step_1_outcome: No OOJ match → fall through to step 2
items.step_2_4_walk: Step 2 (re-grep + minor/info)? No — moderate forbidden, and severity is major. Step 3 (narrowness gate)? No — multi-WIN holistic SC reclassification, not single-line. Step 4 (NEVER_PUSH/physical/legal/operator-applied)? No — analyst can draft EXP. Step 5 (curmudgeon re-attack)? No — next action is analyst EXP drafting, not curmudgeon attack.

items.description_summary: WIN-028 strawman risk: agonic-line mechanism attribution to dome where dome published no mechanism. Analyst writes defense.
items.OOJ_check: OOJ-1? NO — not a verdict commit. OOJ-2? NO — wins.json patch. OOJ-3? NO — internal argument framing, not external comms. OOJ-4? NO — public dome content, no operator-side data needed. OOJ-5? NO. NOO-2 (strawman defense) explicitly covers this.
items.step_1_outcome: No OOJ match → fall through to step 2
items.step_2_4_walk: Steps 2-5 fall-through similar to ISS-1921 (no narrow patch, no NEVER_PUSH, next action is analyst defense not curmudgeon attack).

items.description_summary: WIN-013 Van Camp 2001 DOI returns HTTP 404. Fix: fetch correct DOI, patch detail_evidence.
items.OOJ_check: OOJ-4? NO — public-data verification (CrossRef/DOI resolver), not operator-side knowledge. NOO-1 (citation precision) explicitly covers this. Severity is critical but per the directive, severity ≠ operator-only.
items.step_1_outcome: No OOJ match → fall through to step 2
items.step_2_4_walk: Step 2: severity is critical, wontfix forbidden. Step 3 (narrowness gate): single citation field, but RE-GREP would need the correct DOI first (which requires investigation) — gate fails on NARROWNESS sub-rule because the fix isn't a 2-3-character pinned correction, it's a fetch-then-patch. Step 4 (NEVER_PUSH/physical/legal): wins.json is git-owned, fetch from CrossRef is public data → no. Step 5: next action is analyst verification, not curmudgeon re-attack.

items.description_summary: WIN-013/014 Tomás & Mendoza-Torres 2018 URL resolves to wrong paper (Park et al.). Fix: find correct URL/DOI for 5–15 nT eclipse-geomagnetic claim.
items.OOJ_check: OOJ-4? NO — public-database lookup (search journals for correct paper), not operator-side data. NOO-1 covers.
items.step_1_outcome: No OOJ match → fall through to step 2
items.step_2_4_walk: Same walk as ISS-1944 — citation fix routed to analyst.

items.description_summary: WIN-028 steelman attribution paragraph (same family as ISS-1933). Analyst defense or paragraph reframe.
items.OOJ_check: Same as ISS-1933 — NOO-2 covers (strawman defense / curmudgeon-raised attribution risk).

items.description_summary: WIN-014.c7 H1 ΔB station-specificity in PRED-TIER-3 derivation. Narrow precision patch with named-station cite (Membach/Strasbourg SG sites, ΔB = 10–13 nT).
items.OOJ_check: No OOJ category matches. NOO-3 (narrow-precision wording fix with named source) covers.
items.step_2_4_walk: Step 3 narrowness gate: arguably could pass — the fix is to make the derivation explicit by citing the named stations. But this requires composing a paragraph, not a single-token rename, so likely fails NARROWNESS sub-rule. Step 6 default: ROUTE-TO-ANALYST.

items.description_summary: WIN-014.c7 H2 Crossley 2013 URL has 2022 in path. Either year is wrong (it's 2022) or URL is wrong. Verify and patch.
items.OOJ_check: Same as ISS-1944 — citation precision, public-data verification. NOO-1 covers.
items.step_2_4_walk: Same as ISS-1944 — citation fix routed to analyst.

summary.verdict: PASS — proposed BAU step 1 reproduces operator's manual routing exactly on the canonical test case. Design is ready for operator review.

alternatives_considered_and_rejected.option: Option A — Eliminate the severity-based step 1 entirely across ALL contexts (M1, M3, and BAU 3b)
alternatives_considered_and_rejected.rejected_because: M1 and M3 see a different item population. M1 candidates have already survived earlier triage — they reach M1 because nothing simpler caught them, suggesting they may be genuine operator-territory. M3 candidates are curmudgeon's repeat-flagged items — second-pass attention warranted. The conservative posture for M1/M3 is empirically defensible (PROP-027's design rationale was correct for those contexts). BAU 3b operates on freshly-raised items where description-shape governs routing, not arrival-via-survival. Don't over-correct: tighten BAU 3b only.

alternatives_considered_and_rejected.option: Option B — Add a 'severity-overrides' annotation field on each ISS that operator can pre-set
alternatives_considered_and_rejected.rejected_because: Adds operator burden (manual annotation per ISS) for a check the LLM can do itself given concrete criteria. Defeats the throughput goal. Also creates a stale-state risk: ISS may transition from analyst-class to operator-class as the WIN evolves, and the annotation wouldn't auto-update.

alternatives_considered_and_rejected.option: Option C — Loosen step 1 by adding 'severity AND no-fix-text-in-description' AND-clause
alternatives_considered_and_rejected.rejected_because: Brittle parser dependency. Citation fixes don't always have 'fix' or 'patch' in the description (e.g., ISS-1944 says 'Van Camp DOI 404' with no explicit fix verb). Description-shape detection by keyword grep is the kind of heuristic that fails on the next batch with different wording. Five-category enumerated check is more robust.

alternatives_considered_and_rejected.option: Option D — Defer to severity for default action, override per-issue with rationale (current PROP-027 pattern)
alternatives_considered_and_rejected.rejected_because: First-fire empirical evidence (7/7 over-escalation) shows the override path is being underused. Sonnet defaults to the matrix's literal text and does NOT override toward route-to-analyst even when description shape suggests it. Without enumerated criteria telling Sonnet WHEN to override, the override path is a no-op.

alternatives_considered_and_rejected.option: Option E — Keep step 1 uniform but add a 'description shape pre-filter' before step 1 (auto-route citation/strawman to analyst before severity check)
alternatives_considered_and_rejected.rejected_because: Cleanly factorable but harder to maintain. Adding a 'pre-filter' step inverts the matrix's top-down 'first match wins' clarity. Three-branch step 1 with operator-only criteria preserves the matrix's structural simplicity.

counter_argument_section.prompt_from_directive_task_5_and_user_pr: Operator wants the honest read. Tinker MUST consider 'is the conservative-default-escalate posture actually right and were the 7 items today edge cases?' And the user's standing preference is to think about the other side of the argument too.

arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.argument: Critical citation failures ARE more consequential than minor citation failures — high-severity items deserve human eyes-on review even if mechanically fixable.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.merit: Real concern in spirit but weak in mechanism. Eyes-on review IS happening — just not via escalate-to-pending-human. Analyst is an LLM with a re-verification step (curmudgeon batchability gate); operator reviews the integrated EXP via routine workspace inspection. Escalating critical items to pending-human means they sit unactioned waiting for operator attention, which is SLOWER than analyst processing them. The actual operator-eyes-on signal is closer to 'verdict commit on already-published material' (OOJ-1) than 'severity tier'.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.verdict: Spirit honored by OOJ-1; mechanism rejected. Severity != operator-eyes-on requirement.

arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.argument: Sonnet may inconsistently apply the OOJ check, leading to silent under-escalation of items that genuinely need operator attention.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.merit: Real LLM-behavior risk. Sonnet could rationalize 'this looks like analyst-class' on an item that subtly requires operator-only knowledge. Examples: a citation fix that's actually a credit-attribution dispute (analyst can't resolve a credit fight); a verdict-reclassification proposal that's actually a strategic posture change disguised as a verification update.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.mitigation: Tie-breaker rule (uncertain → escalate). Five concrete OOJ examples + four NOO examples in the prompt set the calibration. The two-week post-landing validation window catches drift. If under-escalation rate (operator manually flips analyst→pending-human) exceeds 1/week, PROP-032 is too loose and needs revision.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.verdict: Real risk, mitigated by tie-breaker + monitoring. Not a blocker.

arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.argument: The 2026-05-12 7-item batch may not generalize. Different ISS populations (e.g., a future cluster of operator-only items) could overwhelm BAU 3b under PROP-032 and silently route to analyst.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.merit: Possible. The 7-item batch happens to skew citation-heavy (3/7 NOO-1) plus strawman-heavy (2/7 NOO-2). A future batch dominated by, say, NEVER_PUSH file modifications would test OOJ-2 differently.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.mitigation: Monitor escalate-rate and analyst-reroute-back rate. If analyst routinely flips items back to pending-human (analyst-detected-OOJ retroactively), refine the criteria. The five OOJ categories cover the directive's enumerated cases; expand if monitoring reveals gaps.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.verdict: Generalizability is a known unknown. Five-category enumeration is the best calibration available from the canonical test case. Iterate if needed.

arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.argument: BAU 3b's rapid throughput on analyst-class items shifts the bottleneck downstream — analyst now has 165 assigned-analyst items (up from ~50 pre-PROP-031). PROP-032 accelerates this.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.merit: True. PROP-032 routes more items to analyst (instead of pending-human, where they'd sit). Net pipeline backlog doesn't disappear, it relocates.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.mitigation: Out of scope for PROP-032 (this PROP fixes routing correctness, not analyst throughput). Analyst-throughput scaling is a separate PROP if needed (e.g., 'analyst Mode 1 dispatcher batch size tuning'). Note: pending-human at 165 would be operator-blocking; assigned-analyst at 165 is queue-processable. Even with analyst as bottleneck, the routing change is correct.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.verdict: Real downstream concern, but PROP-032 is the right routing fix regardless. Analyst scaling is a separable issue.

arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.argument: The directive itself acknowledges 'M1 step 1 conservative posture was correct for the M1 stale-sweep introduction.' Maybe BAU 3b is similarly in its early-deployment period and conservative defaults are still right.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.merit: Plausible. BAU 3b launched 2026-05-11 and its first run (2026-05-12) is two days old. Two-day data is thin.
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.mitigation: But: the operator's review of those 7 items is 7-of-7 unambiguous. A 100% over-escalation rate on the first batch is strong signal — not the kind of 'edge case' a conservative default exists to catch. The operator's framing 'the two criticals don't need human, they just need the citation fixed' is empirically grounded, not preference. M1 and M3 still get the conservative posture preserved (this PROP doesn't touch them).
arguments_FOR_keeping_severity_as_escala.verdict: Two-day data is thin BUT 7/7 unambiguous on a population that is BAU 3b's bread-and-butter (curmudgeon-raised analyst-class items) is sufficient signal. Conservative posture preserved where it makes sense (M1/M3); tightened where it doesn't (BAU 3b).

counter_argument_section.tinker_overall_judgment: The directive's diagnosis is correct: severity is not the right escalation trigger for BAU 3b's item population. The operator-only-judgment criteria are concrete and Sonnet-actionable. Retroactive simulation passes 7/7. Counter-arguments do not invalidate the design, only refine its calibration (tie-breaker, monitoring, future iteration if needed). PROP-032 is the right tightening; supersede is NOT the right outcome.
counter_argument_section.honest_read_recommendation: Land PROP-032. Watch for 2 weeks. If pending-human under-escalation rate stays low (operator manually flipping analyst→pending-human < 1/week), the tightening is calibrated correctly. If analyst routinely re-escalates items as 'I can't resolve this without operator', the OOJ criteria need expansion. The PROP-031 launch had a 100% miscalibration rate — the bar for PROP-032 is just 'better than that', which is a low bar to clear.

what_NOT_to_change.introduction: Per directive task_5: don't over-correct. The conservative posture exists for good reason in M1 and M3 contexts. PROP-032 ONLY revises BAU 3b's step 1.

explicit_preserve_list.PROP_032_status: PRESERVED. M1 catches items that survived all earlier triage; the conservative default is the safety net's reason for existing.

explicit_preserve_list.current_text: severity ∈ {major, critical} AND no prior route action this cycle → ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN. Invariant.
explicit_preserve_list.PROP_032_status: PRESERVED. M3 catches items curmudgeon already flagged twice; second-pass escalation discipline preserved.

PRESERVED. WONTFIX-WITH-RATIONALE forbidden on moderate; PATCH/NARROW-PATCH gated by three-rule narrowness gate; NEVER_PUSH escalate via step 4; ROUTE-TO-CURMUDGEON for adversarial re-attack; ROUTE-TO-ANALYST default. Unchanged.

PRESERVED. M1 → ISS → analyst EXP → queue push → curmudgeon batchability gate. Severity informs class_hint default in all contexts (critical/major usually deep-attack; minor/info usually verification). PROP-032 doesn't touch this.
PRESERVED. action_taken='escalate' covers both severity-based (M1/M3) and OOJ-based (BAU 3b) escalations; closure_evidence.rationale carries the trigger detail. No new fields needed.
PRESERVED. The matrix is still a default; LLM may override per-item with rationale. PROP-032 just changes the default for BAU 3b's step 1.

what_PROP_032_does_NOT_propose: Do NOT add complex severity-AND-category compound logic (keep step 1 single-statement-per-context).
Do NOT replace the routing-matrix.md file structure; only insert the three-branch step 1 above the existing table.
Do NOT modify decider.md beyond the one-line pointer in Priority 3b.
Do NOT change PROP-027 or PROP-031 design — this is a refinement of step 1 within those PROPs' frameworks.
Do NOT add new fields to open-issues.json or closure-ledger.jsonl.
Do NOT touch M1 or M3 invocation paths.

metric_1_BAU_3b_escalate_rate.source: decider daily-report-*.json bau_triage.escalated field; cross-check operator manual reroutes
metric_1_BAU_3b_escalate_rate.target_post_PROP032: 0-1 escalate per BAU 3b run within 1 week; sustained 0-2 per week within 2 weeks
metric_1_BAU_3b_escalate_rate.decision_rule: If escalate-rate stays >20% on any single BAU 3b run after PROP-032 lands, the OOJ criteria are too restrictive — investigate which category mis-fired.

metric_2_operator_manual_reroute_count.source: Operator-side observation; HNOTE pattern; pending-human → assigned-analyst transitions in open-issues.json diff
metric_2_operator_manual_reroute_count.decision_rule: If operator reroutes >2 items per week, BAU step 1 is still mis-calibrated; PROP-032r2 needed.

metric_3_analyst_re_escalation_rate.source: Analyst Mode 2 outputs; ISS status transitions assigned-analyst → pending-human
metric_3_analyst_re_escalation_rate.decision_rule: If analyst routinely re-escalates (>2% rate), OOJ criteria are too LOOSE — items requiring operator-only judgment are slipping through. Add to OOJ enumeration.

metric_4_pending_human_count_drift.decision_rule: If pending-human count grows >10, investigate per-item — may need OOJ category expansion.

post_landing_validation_plan.rollback_criteria: If PROP-032 causes any of: (a) silent loss of an operator-territory item (analyst processes something it shouldn't have, surfaces during routine workspace inspection), (b) escalate-rate stays >50% (PROP-032 didn't actually loosen anything), (c) operator files HNOTE saying 'reroute storm — PROP-032 is wrong', operator flips routing-matrix.md back to single-row step 1 via direct prompt edit (revert is one-line change). Flag in latest-tinker-summary.txt as a tinker-watched metric.

related_proposals.PROP-027: Origin of the 5-action matrix and the severity-based step 1. PROP-032 refines step 1 for BAU context; preserves PROP-027's M1/M3 contexts and steps 2-6 entirely.
related_proposals.PROP-031: Introduced BAU 3b as the third invocation context. PROP-032 is the targeted fix for BAU 3b's step 1 invocation; it does not touch PROP-031's Priority 3b mechanism, scope, or self-test.
related_proposals.PROP-030: Backlog-trend monitoring. PROP-032's metrics (1-4 above) are tinker-monitorable; PROP-030's queue-history.jsonl gains assigned-analyst and pending-human as monitoring inputs.
related_proposals.PROP-028: Verdict-history invariant — basis for OOJ-1 (verdict commits on operator-authored material). PROP-032 honors PROP-028 by recognizing verdict-commit as operator territory.

expected_implementation_path.step_1_operator_review: Operator reads PROP-032, agrees/disagrees with the five OOJ categories and the three-branch step 1 design. Reviews retroactive simulation against the 7-item batch.
expected_implementation_path.step_2_prompt_edit_authorization: Operator marks PROP-032 status='approved-for-self-apply' (or files HNOTE-OPERATOR-APPROVE-PROP-032).
expected_implementation_path.step_3_decider_self_applies_or_tinker_ap: Next decider run (or next tinker run with self-apply empowerment) applies the two prompt edits: (a) routing-matrix.md step 1 restructure + Operator-Only-Judgment Criteria subsection; (b) decider.md Priority 3b one-line pointer addition. Both files are git-owned, agent-pushable.
expected_implementation_path.step_4_first_BAU_3b_run_under_PROP_032: Subsequent decider run executes Priority 3b for the first time under the new step 1. Tinker monitors decider's daily report for the bau_triage.escalated count and per-item closure_evidence.rationale.
expected_implementation_path.step_5_tinker_validation_window: 2-week post-landing validation. Tinker verifies metrics 1-4 from post_landing_validation_plan. If pass: mark PROP-032 status='implemented-and-validated'. If fail per rollback_criteria: flip back to single-row step 1 and re-tinker.
expected_implementation_path.step_6_directive_close_out: On successful validation, DIRECTIVE-20260512-001 marked status='completed' with completed_by_run pointing to the validation tinker run.

writers_and_review_audit.tinker_self_consistency_check: Re-read this PROP top-to-bottom before writing. Verified: each of the 7 retroactive simulation items references the actual ISS description content; the OOJ category for each item is consistent with the directive's enumerated breakdown; the M1/M3 preservation list is concrete and mutually exclusive with BAU 3b changes; counter-arguments include real LLM-behavior risks (Sonnet drift, 100% over-escalation generalizability) not just strawmen; alternatives_considered cover the directive's task_5 ('what NOT to do') instead of just listing my preferred design.
writers_and_review_audit.operator_visibility: This PROP is git-committed alongside the tinker run's normal report and summary files. Workspace-sync universal-pusher will surface the PROP within ~1h, operator sees on next workspace inspection.
